verdict: Across 6 GRU configurations (hidden 16-48, dropout 0.2-0.4, weight decay 1e-5 to 1e-3, K in 4/8/12, 3 seeds), out-of-time PR-AUC ranges 0.189 to 0.246, every config below the served GBM's 0.301; five of six sit inside its bootstrap CI and the sixth (K=4) sits just below it, i.e. even worse. The single-config result is not a tuning artifact: no GRU configuration beats the incumbent at this data scale regardless of capacity, regularization, history length, or seed, and the large in-sample to out-of-time gap (inner-val 0.56-0.67 collapsing to OOT 0.19-0.25) persists across every config, supporting the non-transfer reading.
